trends.name: Collective Desire to Move from Less Harm to More Good
trends.description: A fundamental shift in societal ambition — from minimizing negative impact to actively generating positive change — driven by 8 in 10 people worldwide seriously worried about the future.

trends.name: Polycrisis as Permanent Operating Context
trends.description: The convergence of political instability, cost-of-living pressure, armed conflict, and climate impact creating a sustained polycrisis that reshapes what people expect from brands and institutions.

signals.name: Brands Expected to Close the Say-Do Gap
signals.description: Consumer expectation that brands bridge the gap between stated values and actual behavior — with the say-do gap becoming a primary source of brand trust erosion.

signals.name: Cultural AI Analytics Enabling Human Insight at Scale
signals.description: AI-powered cultural analytics tools enabling brands and researchers to analyze what truly matters to people at population scale — combining AI efficiency with human understanding depth.

trends.name: Home as Contested Space of Tensions
trends.description: The home becoming a site of active negotiation between competing needs — doing more vs. less, togetherness vs. privacy, living well vs. living within means — reflecting broader societal polarities.

trends.name: Life at Home as Wellbeing Indicator
trends.description: Home satisfaction emerging as a primary wellbeing metric — with 60% positive about home life but structural tensions around affordability, privacy, and rest defining the modern domestic experience.

signals.name: Future of Home in 2030 Being Actively Imagined
signals.description: IKEA research surfacing multiple distinct scenarios for home life in 2030 — signaling that the trajectory of domestic life is genuinely uncertain and subject to technological, economic, and social forces.

scenarios.name: Three Tensions for 2023 Home Life
scenarios.description: IKEA scenario framework identifying three defining tensions shaping domestic life in 2023: doing more vs. doing less, togetherness vs. privacy, and living well vs. living within means.

trends.name: Geopolitical and Economic Uncertainty in Tourism
trends.description: Tourism sector navigating compounding geopolitical and macroeconomic uncertainty as a primary business environment challenge in 2024-2025 — requiring adaptive strategy across all hospitality operations.

trends.name: AI Across the CPG Value Chain
trends.description: Consumer packaged goods companies integrating AI across all major business functions — with marketing technology, demand forecasting, and supply chain optimization becoming AI-dependent.

trends.name: Emerging Markets as CPG Growth Engine
trends.description: Emerging markets driving disproportionate CPG growth as their middle classes expand — with 24% growth expected from these markets requiring fundamentally different product and distribution strategies.

signals.name: Resilient Marketing Technology as CPG Priority
signals.description: CPG companies prioritizing resilient, privacy-compliant marketing technology infrastructure as the foundation for AI-driven personalization and first-party data strategies.

trends.name: Generative AI Adoption in Retail and Hospitality Mainstream
trends.description: 80% of retail and hospitality companies already initiating generative AI journeys — with spending growth expected to significantly exceed the already-strong overall enterprise AI trend.

signals.name: GenAI Spending Growing Faster in Retail Than Other Sectors
signals.description: Retail and hospitality generative AI investment growing faster than most other industry sectors, signaling the consumer-facing industries as primary early adopters of GenAI capabilities.

trends.name: 5G Standalone Deployment Accelerating
trends.description: Telecom operators deploying 5G Standalone networks — enabling ultra-low latency applications that 5G Non-Standalone cannot support — ahead of complete 5G coverage rollout, driven by revenue urgency.

trends.name: AI-Native Telecom Operations
trends.description: AI becoming foundational to telecom operations — from network management and customer experience to sales and fraud detection — transforming telecommunications from connectivity to intelligence provider.

signals.name: Edge Computing Becoming Telecom Revenue Opportunity
signals.description: Telecom companies positioning edge computing infrastructure as a new revenue stream — leveraging their distributed network assets to provide compute capacity for latency-sensitive applications.

trends.name: Private Markets Vulnerabilities Entering View
trends.description: Private credit and private equity market vulnerabilities becoming visible as rate environment shifts — with opacity and leverage in private markets emerging as systemic risk concerns for 2025.

signals.name: ECB Rate Cuts Benefiting Bank Loan Quality
signals.description: European Central Bank rate cuts expected to improve bank loan portfolio quality in 2025 — reducing credit costs after elevated levels, benefiting banks with exposure to rate-sensitive borrowers.

signals.name: Sustainable Bank Bond Issuance Slowing
signals.description: Green and sustainable bank bond issuance expected to grow only modestly in 2025, signaling that sustainable finance growth is entering a more measured phase after the initial rush.

trends.name: Banks Navigating Higher-for-Longer Rate Environment
trends.description: European banking sector adapting business models to a structurally higher interest rate environment — with winners and losers emerging based on funding structures, loan book quality, and capital positioning.

signals.name: European Taxonomy Driving Bank Disclosure Expansion
signals.description: EU Taxonomy requirements forcing European banks to dramatically expand environmental and sustainability disclosures — creating significant reporting burden and beginning to shape lending strategy.

signals.name: Nordic Banks Most Exposed to CRE Risk
signals.description: Nordic banking systems showing disproportionate commercial real estate exposure — creating specific vulnerability as property values adjust to higher rate environment.

trends.name: Healthcare Trust Fragmentation
trends.description: Consumers losing confidence in traditional healthcare organizations and turning to family, friends, social media, and online sources for medical information — fragmenting the patient journey away from institutional pathways.

trends.name: Social Media Shaping Health Decisions
trends.description: Social media platforms exerting outsized influence on consumer health decisions — including medication choices, treatment approaches, and health identity — creating both opportunity and misinformation risk.

signals.name: Retail Healthcare Drawing Patients from Traditional Care Path
signals.description: Retail health clinics and pharmacies capturing patients who would previously enter traditional care settings — structurally changing the first point of care for many health conditions.

trends.name: Brand Growth Deceleration After Peak
trends.description: Global brand value growth decelerating sharply — from 16% in 2022 to 5.7% in 2023 — as the post-pandemic premium era ends and brand investment strategies become more cautious and incremental.

trends.name: Leading Across Multiple Brand Arenas
trends.description: The most valuable global brands succeeding by operating across multiple distinct arenas rather than owning a single category — expanding the definition of competitive relevance.

signals.name: C-Suite Playing It Safe on Brand Strategy
signals.description: Interbrand research finding widespread C-suite tendency toward brand risk avoidance — driving incremental rather than transformative investment and contributing to deceleration in brand value creation.
